title: Expand tier-3-systems Constitutional Corpus with Openly Licensed Governance Sources
summary: Add 7 openly licensed documents to tier-3-systems to address critical gaps identified in the corpus gap analysis (#67). Current tier-3 has 4 files (200 KB, ~90% theoretical). This proposal adds practical governance playbooks, token economics frameworks, governance failure analysis, and security patterns — all under CC0, CC BY 4.0, or WTFPL licenses.
rationale: The CEO strategic review (2026-03-28) and corpus gap analysis identified that tier-3-systems is 17x smaller than tier-1 and 7x smaller than tier-2, with zero coverage in operational governance, token economics, and governance failure post-mortems. The Algorithmic Manager cannot ground business strategy and operational recommendations in constitutional sources without practical governance literature. Per Meadows leverage point 3: the corpus shapes the AM's goal-setting capacity.

proposedChanges.description: MakerDAO Governance Manual — operational governance playbook, stability fee revenue model, delegate frameworks, treasury management
Vitalik Buterin: Moving Beyond Coin Voting Governance — critique of coin voting, alternatives (proof-of-personhood, quadratic voting, futarchy)
Kivilo et al.: Designing a Token Economy — Token Economy Design Method (TEDM) covering incentives, governance, and tokenomics
Morrison et al.: The DAO Controversy — canonical governance failure case study ($250M crowdfund, $60M exploit, hard fork decision)
Vitalik Buterin: Governance, Part 2: Plutocracy Is Still Bad — critique of plutocratic voting, DPOS scandals, vote-buying dynamics
ENS DAO Governance Documentation — proposal lifecycle, working groups, constitutional framework, registration fee revenue model
ethereum.org Governance Documentation — Ethereum's off-chain governance model, EIP process, rough consensus, social layer governance

excludedSources.reason: No license — standard copyright. Would require direct permission.

excludedSources.source: Trail of Bits: Building Secure Contracts
excludedSources.reason: AGPLv3 — copyleft obligations create legal gray area for corpus inclusion. Deferred pending legal review.

excludedSources.source: a16z blog posts and DAO Canon
excludedSources.reason: Standard copyright (Andreessen Horowitz). No open license.

excludedSources.source: Open Problems in DAOs (Tan et al., arXiv)
excludedSources.reason: arXiv non-exclusive license — does not grant third-party redistribution rights

impactAssessment.theoreticalPracticalBalance: Current: ~90% theoretical / ~10% practical. After: estimated ~50% theoretical / ~50% practical

implementationSteps: Download and convert each source to plain text
Add attribution headers to each file citing license and source URL
Update corpus/manifest.json with new file hashes
Update corpus/weights.json if tier-3 weight adjustment is warranted
Run verify.py --corpus-integrity to confirm manifest consistency
Commit and record on-chain via governance pipeline
